problem: The client requires one durable, authenticated and serviceable delivery-address book that remains authoritative across cart, checkout and application restarts without local address truth, duplicate active records or repeated side effects after uncertain network outcomes.
goal: Persist, read, update, default and soft-delete client-owned delivery addresses through DSH, with durable idempotency, optimistic concurrency, governed service-area validation, privacy controls, committed readback, accessible Arabic UX and PII-free operational diagnostics.

actors.scope: governed aggregate diagnostics and privacy actions only
persistence, validation, mutation receipts, events and checkout resolution

excludedSurfaces.reason: Partners do not own client delivery-address books.
Field staff do not create or mutate client-owned addresses.
Captains receive order-scoped delivery snapshots only after order creation.
No authenticated client address-book runtime is registered on the public website.

acceptanceCriteria: Authenticated clients can list only their active addresses.
Every create, update, delete and set-default mutation is retry-safe and exactly-once for a client-scoped idempotency key.
A committed update replay is resolved before mutable service-area validation.
Reusing an idempotency key for a different request returns IDEMPOTENCY_CONFLICT.
An active logical duplicate is rejected with ADDRESS_ALREADY_EXISTS.
Update, delete and set-default reject stale versions with ADDRESS_CONFLICT.
Exactly one active address is default whenever active addresses exist.
Deleting the default deterministically promotes one remaining active address and records the promotion event.
Checkout resolves a client-owned address by deliveryAddressId and never accepts a client-supplied snapshot as truth.
Coordinates and serviceAreaCode are verified by DSH and the PostgreSQL geofence trigger before persistence.
Soft-deleted address PII follows the governed retention and anonymization policy.
Mutation receipts contain no address PII and expire through a bounded 30-day purge policy.
The client validates the OpenAPI field bounds, requires governed coordinates and refreshes committed state after mutation uncertainty.
The Arabic address screen exposes labelled bounded inputs, live errors, busy states and delete confirmation.
Operational telemetry and diagnostics expose aggregate outcomes and integrity counts without address or actor identifiers.
No surface persists address records in AsyncStorage, localStorage, sessionStorage, MMKV, fixtures or mocks.
negativeInvariants: No clientId query parameter may select an address owner.
No cross-client read, update, default or delete is permitted and failed cross-client attempts may not create receipts.
No second active default or active logical duplicate is permitted.
No unverified or mismatched service-area coordinates may be committed.
No mutation may increment versions or emit domain events twice for a matching replay.
No mutation success may be displayed before canonical response and readback.
No address PII may be emitted in logs, telemetry, diagnostics, metrics labels, mutation receipts or correlation identifiers.
No mutation receipt may remain unbounded beyond the governed retention policy.
decisionPolicy: READY_FOR_REVIEW requires all FS-01..FS-18 code checks on one commit. CLOSED_WITH_EVIDENCE additionally requires independent Product, QA, Security, Privacy, Accessibility, physical-device and Release evidence.
